seo.title: Building Customer Support with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Pydantic AI enables building robust, type-safe AI agents for customer support, handling RAG retrieval, CRM integrations, and tool calls for ticket creation and order lookups. Guardrails are essential due to high-stakes interactions involving PII, policy compliance, and escalation decisions. Vex provides production-grade verification to catch validation failures, tone inconsistencies, and persistent tool errors before they impact customers.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on a RAG-enabled agent that processes customer queries through a structured pipeline. Incoming support tickets trigger knowledge base retrieval using vector search, followed by LLM reasoning with Pydantic models for structured outputs. The agent orchestrates tools for CRM lookups (order status, customer history) and ticket creation/updates, with result validators ensuring data integrity.

Tool calling handles dynamic workflows like escalation triggers and PII redaction, while streaming responses maintain real-time chat UX. Dependency injection provides CRM context and knowledge base access to nested tool calls. Vex integrates as result validators to catch common failures like validator rejections and missing DI context.

The high-risk profile demands verification for response accuracy, PII filtering, tone consistency, policy compliance, and proper escalation routing to human agents.

risks.name: Result validator rejecting valid but unexpected outputs
risks.description: Pydantic models reject semantically correct responses that don't match exact schemas, causing unnecessary failures
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic verification validates intent before strict schema enforcement

risks.name: PII leakage in customer responses
risks.description: Unfiltered PII from CRM data appears in agent responses despite redaction attempts
risks.mitigation: Vex PII detection scanner blocks responses containing sensitive identifiers

risks.name: Tone inconsistency violating brand guidelines
risks.description: Agent responses vary from professional/supportive tone to casual/inappropriate
risks.mitigation: Vex tone analysis ensures consistent brand voice across interactions

risks.name: Missed escalation triggers
risks.description: High-priority issues (billing disputes, outages) not properly routed to human agents
risks.mitigation: Vex policy checker validates escalation logic before response delivery

implementation.title: Core RAG Agent with Pydantic Models
implementation.description: Define the customer support agent with knowledge base retrieval and structured response models
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, Tool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List

class SupportResponse(BaseModel):
    summary: str = Field(..., description="Concise issue summary")
    resolution_steps: List[str]
    confidence: float
    needs_escalation: bool = Field(..., description="Route to human agent")

class CustomerSupportAgent(Agent):
    model = 'gpt-4o'
    response_model = SupportResponse
    
    @Tool()
    async def retrieve_kb(self, query: str) -> str:
        """Retrieve relevant knowledge base articles"""
        # Vector search implementation
        return "KB results for: " + query
    
    @Tool()
    async def lookup_order(self, order_id: str) -> dict:
        """Fetch order status from CRM"""
        return {'status': 'shipped', 'tracking': 'UPS123'}

agent = CustomerSupportAgent()

implementation.title: CRM Integration Tools
implementation.description: Implement production CRM tools for order lookup and ticket creation
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Tool
from typing import Optional

class CRMTools:
    
    @Tool()
    async def get_customer_history(self, customer_id: str) -> dict:
        """Retrieve complete customer interaction history"""
        # CRM API call
        return {
            'tickets': 3,
            'last_interaction': '2024-01-15',
            'tier': 'premium',
            'pii_filtered': True
        }
    
    @Tool()
    async def create_ticket(self, customer_id: str, issue: str, priority: str = 'medium') -> str:
        """Create support ticket in CRM"""
        ticket_id = f"TKT-{customer_id}-{hash(issue) % 10000}"
        # CRM ticket creation
        return f"Ticket {ticket_id} created with priority {priority}"

implementation.title: Streaming Structured Responses
implementation.description: Enable real-time chat with structured output validation
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from pydantic import BaseModel

class StreamingSupportAgent(Agent):
    model = 'gpt-4o'
    stream = True
    
    async def handle_query(self, query: str):
        async for chunk in self.run(query):
            if hasattr(chunk, 'response_model'):
                yield chunk.response_model.dict()
            else:
                yield chunk
    
agent = StreamingSupportAgent()

# Usage
async def chat_handler(message: str):
    async for response in agent.handle_query(message):
        print(response)

implementation.description: Provide CRM context to nested tool calls

vexIntegration.title: Vex Result Validator on Agent Definition
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as primary result validator to catch schema rejections and semantic issues
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from pydantic import BaseModel

# Initialize Vex client
vex = Vex(api_key="vex_12345", project_id="cs-pydantic-ai")

class VerifiedSupportAgent(Agent):
    model = 'gpt-4o'
    
    @vex.result_validator(
        checks=[
            "response_accuracy",
            "pii_filtering",
            "tone_consistency"
        ],
        risk_profile="high"
    )
    async def validate_support_response(self, response: BaseModel) -> bool:
        """Vex validates before Pydantic schema enforcement"""
        result = await vex.verify({
            'response': response.model_dump(),
            'context': 'customer_support',
            'checks': ['accuracy', 'pii', 'tone']
        })
        return result.passed
    
agent = VerifiedSupportAgent()

vexIntegration.title: PII Filtering Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Block PII leakage with Vex entity recognition
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from pydantic_ai import Tool

vex = Vex(api_key="vex_12345")

@vex.guardrail(
    check="pii_detection",
    block_on_fail=True,
    entities=["PHONE_NUMBER", "EMAIL", "SSN"]
)
@Tool()
async def safe_customer_response(customer_data: dict, response: str) -> str:
    """Generate safe customer response with PII filtering"""
    # LLM generates response
    safe_response = await vex.scrub_pii(response)
    return safe_response

# Usage in agent
agent.tools.append(safe_customer_response)
